title: VNC does not work
author: zgabi
body_snippet: I tried to connet to a VNC server and I got the following error: I imported the VNC connection from "original" mRemote version 1.76.20.24615.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Hi @zgabi, thanks for reporting this!

We've done significant work on the VNC protocol recently (color depth, keep-alive, Caps Lock, connection timeouts). The error you're seeing might be related to the import from v1.76.20 — some VNC connection properties may not map correctly to the new version.

Could you help us debug this?
1. **What VNC server** are you connecting to? (TightVNC, RealVNC, UltraVNC, other? And version?)
2. **Could you copy the text of the error message** from the dialog? (scr...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Thanks for the detailed info @zgabi!

The error `Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'stream')` is coming from inside the VncSharpCore library, which handles the VNC protocol. This suggests the TCP connection to TightVNC 2.8.11 either fails to establish or the server rejects the connection before a stream is created.

A few things to check:
1. **Port**: Is your VNC server on the default port **5900**? (Check the connection properties in mRemoteNG)
2. **Firewall**: Can you telnet/ping the VNC port f...
